intro: Xylon rivieri, also known as Xylose or Xylose tree, is a type of deciduous tree native to tropical regions of Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Xylon rivieri include xylose, a type of sugar, as well as other compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
